story.text: The reflector's owner-ask mirror double-fires (#547): within hours #525-#544 duplicated #511-#530, each report's ask group surfacing 2-3x and burying bdo's real asks under duplicates -- the exact opposite of the owner surface 'the queue refuses to flood you' promises. The fold-level idempotence in loop/reflect.py ((g['id'],'open') not in seen) is real but insufficient under the parallel-worktree fleet: the reflection 'open' ack lives on the LOCAL log, a sibling session forks from main BEFORE the ack exists, each opens its own mirror issue and appends a surface.reflected event with the same content-derived id but a different external_ref; .gitattributes union-merges the logs, so the fold keeps the first ack while GitHub keeps BOTH issues. Proven on the live log: evt.41b3ab81eb04 carries two distinct issue URLs (#200 and #211). The local ack cannot guard a fork that predates it; the shared truth is the surface itself. The fix (.claude/skills/reflect/reflect.py, where outward reach already lives -- never in loop/) stamps each minted mirror with a hidden, stable key (the group's atom_id: report_id for owner-ask, the atom version for the stamp queue, the label for divergences, 'daily-digest' for the digest) and, before minting any open, reads the surface's currently-open mirrors once and declines to mint a second for a key already shown -- recording the ack against the existing issue instead. Level-triggered, kind-agnostic, no second engine. Teeth (SS10, tests/test_reflect.py + tests/test_owner_asks.py): an already-mirrored group mints ZERO duplicates while an empty surface mints exactly one (the paired control), and a failed surface read refuses to mint blind rather than guess. Proven non-vacuous: with the dedup skip removed the already-mirrored test FAILS ('minted a duplicate for an already-mirrored group'). Value: bdo's desk stays readable -- one open issue per ask group, the favorite-customer experience the digest exists to give, not a flood of dup tickets.
